segments.text: Jehoshaphat had riches and honor in abundance; and by marriage he allied himself with Ahab.
After some years he went down to visit Ahab in Samaria; and Ahab killed sheep and oxen in abundance for him and the people who were with him, and persuaded him to go up with him to Ramoth Gilead.
So Ahab king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat king of Judah,
Will you go with me against Ramoth Gilead?
segments.speaker: Ahab

segments.text: And he answered him,
I am as you are, and my people as your people; we will be with you in the war.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Also Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel,
Please inquire for the word of the Lord today.
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together, four hundred men, and said to them,
Shall we go to war against Ramoth Gilead, or shall I refrain?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: So they said,
Go up, for God will deliver it into the king's hand.
segments.speaker: Prophets

segments.text: But Jehoshaphat said,
Is there not still a prophet of the Lord here, that we may inquire of Him?
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: So the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat,
There is still one man by whom we may inquire of the Lord; but I hate him, because he never prophesies good concerning me, but always evil. He is Micaiah the son of Imla.
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: And Jehoshaphat said,
Let not the king say such things!
segments.speaker: Jehoshaphat

segments.text: Then the king of Israel called one of his officers and said,
Bring Micaiah the son of Imla quickly!
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, clothed in their robes, sat each on his throne; and they sat at a threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets prophesied before them.
Now Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah had made horns of iron for himself; and he said,
With these you shall gore the Syrians until they are destroyed.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And all the prophets prophesied so, saying,
Go up to Ramoth Gilead and prosper, for the Lord will deliver it into the king's hand.
segments.speaker: Prophets

segments.text: Then the messenger who had gone to call Micaiah spoke to him, saying,
Now listen, the words of the prophets with one accord encourage the king. Therefore please let your word be like the word of one of them, and speak encouragement.
segments.speaker: Messenger

segments.text: And Micaiah said,
As the Lord lives, whatever my God says, that I will speak.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Then he came to the king; and the king said to him,
Micaiah, shall we go to war against Ramoth Gilead, or shall I refrain?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: And he said,
Go and prosper, and they shall be delivered into your hand!
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: So the king said to him,
How many times shall I make you swear that you tell me nothing but the truth in the name of the Lord?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: Then he said,
I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains, as sheep that have no shepherd.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: These have no master. Let each return to his house in peace.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat,
Did I not tell you he would not prophesy good concerning me, but evil?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: Then Micaiah said,
Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on His throne, and all the host of heaven standing on His right hand and His left.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Who will persuade Ahab king of Israel to go up, that he may fall at Ramoth Gilead?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So one spoke in this manner, and another spoke in that manner.
Then a spirit came forward and stood before the Lord, and said,
I will persuade him.
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: In what way?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So he said,
I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: You shall persuade him and also prevail; go out and do so.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Therefore look! The Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouth of these prophets of yours, and the Lord has declared disaster against you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah went near and struck Micaiah on the cheek, and said,
Which way did the spirit from the Lord go from me to speak to you?
segments.speaker: Zedekiah

segments.text: And Micaiah said,
Indeed you shall see on that day when you go into an inner chamber to hide!
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Then the king of Israel said,
Take Micaiah, and return him to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king's son;
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: and say,
Thus says the king: Put this fellow in prison, and feed him with bread of affliction and water of affliction, until I return in peace.
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: But Micaiah said,
If you ever return in peace, the Lord has not spoken by me.
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: Take heed, all you people!
segments.speaker: Micaiah

segments.text: So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth Gilead.
And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat,
I will disguise myself and go into battle; but you put on your robes.
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: So the king of Israel disguised himself, and they went into battle.
Now the king of Syria had commanded the captains of the chariots who were with him, saying,
Fight with no one small or great, but only with the king of Israel.
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: So it was, when the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, that they said,
It is the king of Israel!
segments.speaker: Chariot Captains

segments.text: Therefore they surrounded him to attack; but Jehoshaphat cried out, and the Lord helped him, and God diverted them from him.
For so it was, when the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Israel, that they turned back from pursuing him.
Now a certain man drew a bow at random, and struck the king of Israel between the joints of his armor. So he said to the driver of his chariot,
Turn around and take me out of the battle, for I am wounded.
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: The battle increased that day, and the king of Israel propped himself up in his chariot facing the Syrians until evening; and about the time of sunset he died.
